summary: Conjecture 2 from arXiv:1912.01570 — that fvs(G) ≤ 2·fp(G) for every planar graph G — is a strengthening of Jones' conjecture (the cp-version fvs ≤ 2·cp) and appears to remain open. Related progress since 2019 concerns Jones' conjecture itself (cycle-packing, not face-packing): arXiv:2401.07376 (2024) proves fvs ≤ 2·cp for Halin graphs and 'based planar graphs,' but since fp(G) ≤ cp(G), this does not imply the face-packing conjecture. No paper resolving Conjecture 2 was found in the indexed literature.

since_posted.title: Jones' conjecture for Halin graphs and a bit more
since_posted.claim: Proves Jones' conjecture (fvs ≤ 2·cp) for Halin graphs and based planar graphs, but addresses cycle-packing (cp) rather than face-packing (fp), so does not directly settle Conjecture 2.

search_queries: face-packing feedback vertex set planar graph fvs fp conjecture 1912.01570
"face-packing" "feedback vertex set" planar graph conjecture 2020 2021 2022 2023 2024 2025 arxiv
notes: Conjecture 2 (fvs ≤ 2·fp) is strictly stronger than Jones' conjecture (fvs ≤ 2·cp) because any face-packing is a cycle-packing so fp(G) ≤ cp(G). The best known general bound remains fvs(G) ≤ 3·cp(G). The 2401.07376 paper (2024) makes progress on Jones' conjecture for special graph classes but not on the face-packing variant. Author names for 2401.07376 could not be confirmed from the fetched abstract page.
